title: Sync Event Sources Response
description: Response from event source sync operation. Returns either per-source results OR operation-level errors.

oneOf.title: SyncEventSourcesSuccess
oneOf.description: Success response - sync operation processed event sources

event_sources.description: Results for each event source, including both synced and seller-managed sources on the account

event_source_id.description: Event source ID from the request

seller_id.description: Seller-assigned identifier for this event source (the ID in the seller's ad platform)

items.title: Event Type
items.description: Standard marketing event types for event logging, aligned with IAB ECAPI

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

action_source.title: Action Source
action_source.description: Type of event source (website pixel, app SDK, in-store, etc.)

enumDescriptions.app: Event occurred in a mobile or desktop app
enumDescriptions.phone_call: Event originated from a phone call
enumDescriptions.chat: Event originated from a chat conversation
enumDescriptions.email: Event originated from an email interaction
enumDescriptions.in_store: Event occurred at a physical retail location
enumDescriptions.system_generated: Event generated by an automated system

managed_by.description: Who manages this event source. 'buyer' = configured via this sync. 'seller' = always-on, managed by the seller (e.g. Amazon sales attribution for Amazon advertisers).

setup.description: Implementation details for activating this event source (e.g. JavaScript tag, pixel URL)

snippet.description: Code snippet to place on the site (JavaScript, HTML pixel, etc.)

snippet_type.description: Type of implementation. 'server_only' means no client-side tag is needed.

action.description: Action taken for this event source

health.title: Event Source Health
health.description: Health assessment for this event source. Reflects event volume, data quality, and parameter completeness. Sellers that support health scoring include this on every source (buyer-managed and seller-managed). Absent when the seller does not evaluate event source health.

status.title: Assessment Status
status.description: Overall health status. Use this for cross-seller decisions — do not rely on detail.score for comparability.

enumDescriptions.insufficient: Setup incomplete or event quality too low — optimization cannot run
enumDescriptions.minimum: Functional but data quality limits optimization effectiveness
enumDescriptions.good: Meets quality thresholds for most optimization goals
enumDescriptions.excellent: Exceeds quality thresholds across all dimensions

detail.description: Seller-specific scoring detail. Only present when the seller has a native quality score to relay. Buyer agents should use status (not detail) for cross-seller decisions. Detail is supplementary context for human review or advanced diagnostics.

score.description: Seller-defined quality score. Scale varies by seller — only compare within the same seller.

max_score.description: Maximum possible score on this seller's scale.

label.description: Seller's name for this score (e.g., 'Event Quality Score', 'Event Match Quality').

match_rate.description: Fraction of events from this source that the seller successfully matched to ad interactions (0.0-1.0). Low match rates indicate weak user_match identifiers. Absent when the seller does not compute match rates.

last_event_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent event received from this source. Absent when no events have been received.

evaluated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this health assessment was computed. When health is derived from reporting data, this may lag real-time. Buyer agents can use this to decide whether to trust stale assessments or re-request.

events_received_24h.description: Number of events received from this source in the last 24 hours. Zero indicates the source is configured but not firing.

issues.description: Actionable issues detected with this event source. Sellers should limit to the top 3-5 most actionable items. Buyer agents should sort by severity rather than relying on array position.

items.title: Diagnostic Issue
items.description: An actionable issue detected during a health or readiness assessment. Used by event source health and measurement readiness to surface problems and recommendations.

severity.description: 'error': blocks optimization until resolved. 'warning': optimization works but effectiveness is reduced. 'info': suggestion for improvement.

message.description: Human/agent-readable description of the issue and how to resolve it.

errors.description: Errors for this event source (only present when action='failed')

sandbox.description: When true, this response contains simulated data from sandbox mode.

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

items.title: Error
items.description: Standard error structure for task-specific errors and warnings

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Standard codes are defined in error-code.json and enable autonomous agent recovery. Sellers MAY use codes not in the standard vocabulary for platform-specific errors; agents MUST handle unknown codes gracefully by falling back to the recovery classification.

field.description: Field path associated with the error (e.g., 'packages[0].targeting')

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying the operation. Sellers MUST return values between 1 and 3600. Clients MUST clamp values outside this range.

recovery.description: Agent recovery classification. transient: retry after delay (rate limit, service unavailable, timeout). correctable: fix the request and resend (invalid field, budget too low, creative rejected). terminal: requires human action (account suspended, payment required, account not found).

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
